text: Explain how photosynthesis works in simple terms.

prompt_transcript: The future is wide open Hal. You're handsome, smart, and people believe in you. You can do everything you want.
text: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to turn sunlight into food. They absorb sunlight through their leaves and use it to convert carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil into glucose, a type of sugar that provides them energy. Oxygen is released as a byproduct.

text: What are some nutritious vegetarian sources of protein?

prompt_transcript: The future is wide open Hal. You're handsome, smart, and people believe in you. You can do everything you want.
text: Nutritious vegetarian protein sources include lentils, chickpeas, tofu, tempeh, quinoa, nuts, and seeds. Dairy products like Greek yogurt and cottage cheese also provide good protein if you include dairy in your diet.

text: Explain Newton's First Law of Motion.

prompt_transcript: The future is wide open Hal. You're handsome, smart, and people believe in you. You can do everything you want.
text: Newton's First Law of Motion, also known as the law of inertia, states that an object will remain at rest or in uniform motion in a straight line unless acted upon by an external force. This means things stay as they are—whether moving or still—until something causes them to change.
